commitments.text: China will develop the National Strategy for Climate Change Adaptation 2035 (hereinafter referred to as Adaptation Strategy 2035) based on experiences in post-2015 policy practice.
China will further propose and deepen actions on climate change adaptation in terms of improving the climate resilience of natural ecosystems and economic and social systems.
China will develop the Adaptation Strategy 2035, which strengthens the integration of adaptation actions with the goals of economic and social development and beautiful China, and makes work plan.

measurability.reason: Most commitments lack specific targets, units, or baselines.

gap_findings.description: China's NDC does not explicitly address the need for finance mechanisms to address loss and damage.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 17: loss and damage is unavoidable even with adaptation; finance mechanisms urgently needed.

gap_findings.description: China's NDC commitments on loss and damage are vague and lack quantification.

gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 17: loss and damage is unavoidable even with adaptation; finance mechanisms urgently needed.

gap_findings.description: China's NDC commitments on loss and damage are inconsistent with IPCC recommendations, which emphasize the need for finance mechanisms to address loss and damage.

details.summary: China's NDC commitments on loss and damage are vague and lack quantification, and are inconsistent with IPCC recommendations, which emphasize the need for finance mechanisms to address loss and damage.

pass_c.country_summary: China's NDC commitments on loss and damage are vague and lack quantification, and are inconsistent with IPCC recommendations, which emphasize the need for finance mechanisms to address loss and damage.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: China should strengthen its NDC commitments on loss and damage by explicitly addressing the need for finance mechanisms to address loss and damage and providing clear and quantifiable targets.
